Question:
                                How many hours did I spend watching documentaries on Netflix last month?

                                Question date:
                                2023/05/23 (Tue) 17:55

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                10

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            If the user asks about an initial, previous, older, or otherwise historical state, non-current typed memory and older evidence may be the direct answer; compare it with current active memory instead of blindly preferring the current value.
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:311778f1
            Answer mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-14 [ACTIVE] current_metric_value family=current_metric_value: The user's Netflix documentary screen time last month was around 10 hours.; scope=User's self-reported Netflix documentary screen-time benchmark for the month before 2023-05-22.; context="The user estimated spending about 10 hours last month watching documentaries on Netflix."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="I spent around 10 hours last month watching documentaries"
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-13 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants help tracking Netflix screen time.; scope=User's screen-time monitoring goal for Netflix.; context="The user asked for help tracking their Netflix screen time after estimating they spent 10 hours last month watching documentaries."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="I was wondering if you could help me track my screen time on Netflix"
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-8 [ACTIVE] works_with family=works_with: The user watches content on Netflix.; scope=User media-service usage.; context="The user mentioned watching many documentaries on Netflix and later discussed tracking Netflix screen time."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="I've also been watching a lot of documentaries on Netflix"
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-10 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user watches documentaries about artificial intelligence.; scope=User documentary topics and learning interests.; context="The user described watching Netflix documentaries, especially about space exploration and artificial intelligence."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="documentaries on Netflix, especially on space exploration and artificial intelligence"
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-9 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user watches documentaries about space exploration.; scope=User documentary topics and learning interests.; context="The user described watching Netflix documentaries, especially about space exploration and artificial intelligence."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="documentaries on Netflix, especially on space exploration and artificial intelligence"
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-11 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has watched and learned from the series Cosmos.; scope=User media experience and learning history.; context="The user mentioned learning a lot from the series Cosmos while discussing educational documentaries."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="it's crazy how much I learned from that series "Cosmos""
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-25 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user may try reducing screen time by 30 minutes a day.; scope=User's tentative screen-time reduction goal.; context="The user said setting goals and tracking progress would be key, then proposed trying to reduce screen time by 30 minutes a day."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="Maybe I'll try to reduce my screen time by 30 minutes a day and see how that goes."
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-23 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to automate the process of creating visualizations for customer behavior data such as churn prediction and customer segmentation.; scope=User's customer analytics visualization automation goal.; context="The user identified automating visualizations for customer behavior data as their specific use case for deep learning and visualization libraries."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="I'm interested in automating the process of creating visualizations for customer behavior data, such as churn prediction and customer segmentation."
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-12 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to apply machine learning to work projects, especially to automate tasks and make predictions.; scope=User's work-related machine learning goals.; context="The user said they are interested in applying machine learning to work projects and specified automation and prediction as focuses."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="I'm really interested in applying machine learning to my work projects, especially in automating tasks and making predictions."
- claim hot-path-606d2bc340a8-claim-15 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to automate data cleaning.; scope=User's work-related automation goals.; context="The user listed data cleaning as a task they are interested in automating with machine learning."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="I'm interested in automating tasks such as data cleaning and data visualization"

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-606d2bc340a8-note-5 [PLAN/ACTIVE]: Netflix documentary screen-time reduction approach; The user is tracking Netflix documentary screen time after estimating about 10 hours watched last month, and is considering reducing screen time by 30 minutes per day. They view goal setting and progress tracking as key to making the reduction work.; scope=User's personal screen-time tracking and reduction plan for Netflix documentary viewing; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 quote="Maybe I'll try to reduce my screen time by 30 minutes a day and see how that goes."

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:df91421e31e814f87f2f1c3a72d300c1 [external_record ref=longmemeval:311778f1:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/22 (Mon) 12:21
Transcript:
user: I've been consuming a lot of educational content lately, and I'm curious to know, can you recommend some more online courses or resources on data science and machine learning? By the way, I've also been watching a lot of documentaries on Netflix, especially on space exploration and artificial intelligence - it's crazy how much I learned from that series "Cosmos"!...
...3. **Machine Learning Mastery**: A blog with practical tutorials and guides on machine learning.
4. **DataCamp - Data Science Blog**: Articles, tutorials, and news on data science and machine learning....
...user: I'm really interested in applying machine learning to my work projects, especially in automating tasks and making predictions. By the way, speaking of watching educational content, I was wondering if you could help me track my screen time on Netflix - I think I spent 10 hours last month watching documentaries. Can you help me with that?
assistant: Applying machine learning to your work projects sounds like an exciting goal!

Regarding tracking your Netflix screen time, I'm happy to help. Netflix doesn't provide a direct way to track screen time, but I can offer a few workarounds:

**Method 1: Netflix Viewing History**...
...3. Scroll down to the "Settings" section and click on "Viewing activity."
4. You'll see a list of everything you've watched, including the date and time. You can use this to estimate your screen time.

**Method 2: Third-Party Browser Extensions**

1. Install a browser extension like **Netflix Tracker** (Chrome) or **Netflix Watch History** (Firefox).
2. These extensions will track your Netflix viewing history, including the time spent watching....
...1. If you watch Netflix on your mobile device, you can use apps like **Screen Time** (iOS) or **Digital Wellbeing** (Android) to track your screen time.
2. These apps can provide a more accurate picture of your overall screen time, including Netflix usage.

**Method 4: Manual Tracking**

1. Keep a log or spreadsheet to manually track your Netflix screen time.
2. Each time you finish watching a documentary or show, note down the time spent watching.
3. This method requires discipline, but it's a simple way to keep track of your screen time.

To estimate your screen time for last month, you can try Method 1 or Method 2. If you're concerned about your screen time in general, Method 3 or Method 4 might be a better fit....
...By the way, I was also thinking about my Netflix screen time tracking, and I realized that I spent around 10 hours last month watching documentaries, which is a bit more than I expected. I'm glad I can track it now, and maybe I'll try to reduce my screen time in the coming months....
...Regarding your Netflix screen time tracking, it's great that you're taking steps to monitor and manage your screen time! Reducing screen time can have numerous benefits, including improved mental and physical health, increased productivity, and more time for other activities....
...2. **Schedule Screen-Free Time**: Designate specific times of the day or week as screen-free, such as during meals or before bed....
...4. **Use Screen Time Tracking Tools**: Utilize tools, such as Freedom, Moment, or Screen Time, to track your screen time and receive reminders to take breaks.

Remember, small changes can add up over time. Start with small goals, and gradually work your way towards reducing your screen time....
...Thanks for the tips on reducing screen time, by the way. I think setting goals and tracking progress will be key for me. Maybe I'll try to reduce my screen time by 30 minutes a day and see how that goes....
...1. **Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)**: Can be used for sentiment analysis, where the input is a sentence or document, and the output is the sentiment (positive, negative, or neutral)....

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [CONCEPT]: Artificial intelligence; Concept named Artificial intelligence.
- entity entity:<1> [CONCEPT]: Space exploration; Concept named Space exploration.
- entity entity:<2> [PRODUCT]: Cosmos; Product named Cosmos.
- entity entity:<3> [SERVICE]: Netflix; Service named Netflix.
- entity entity:<4> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.
